name: Bloch Sphere
summary: A geometric picture of a single-qubit state that helps map amplitudes and relative phase onto a sphere.
whenToUseIt: Use it when reasoning about single-qubit gates, superposition, phase, and measurement basis changes.
steps: Treat the qubit as a point on the sphere surface when the state is pure.
Interpret rotations as single-qubit gate action.
Track how phase changes move the state around the equator.
Remember that noise can pull the state inward toward a mixed state.
example: A Hadamard gate maps |0> from the north pole onto an equatorial superposition state, making phase relationships visible in a geometric way.
